_NOTE: Auth-blocked baseline capture for #957. Test PC Chrome session is NOT logged into genspark.ai, so /api/agent/ask_proxy returned 401 Unauthorized instead of an SSE stream. Schema fields below were observed via DevTools Console fetch test (shape verified) and from prior iteration knowledge — actual SSE chunks could not be captured.

auth_state.cookies_observed_genspark_domain_only_te: ai_user, ai_session, sidebar_expanded, agree_terms, gslogin=0, snexid, snexid_r, _twpid, _yjsu_yjad, _gcl_au, _ga, _tt_enable_cookie, _ttp, _clck, __stripe_mid, __stripe_sid, _ga_VL3SBJOYOF, g_state, ttcsid, _uetsid, _uetvid, _clsk (all telemetry/marketing — NO auth/session token)

har_observed.har_export_caveat: DevTools 'Export HAR (sanitized)' captured only currently-filtered visible entries (Network filter was set to 'track' from prior session). HAR contains 3 unrelated tver.jp ad-tracking entries. The Console-issued fetch to ask_proxy was visible in Console (status 401) but not preserved in the exported HAR because export ran after page navigated to accounts.google.com OAuth flow.

remediation_required_dev_side: Option A: User logs into genspark.ai on test PC Chrome (Google or Apple account) once — session persists for future captures
Option B: Provide pre-existing genspark session cookies (sessionid, csrf_token, etc.) for injection
Option C: Use a different already-authenticated genspark account on the test PC
comparison_baseline_for_4_prior_FAILs: Cannot establish — auth must be resolved first. Suggest re-issuing #957 after test PC user manually logs in once.
